label: unified-router-schema
prompt: Write ONLY PostgreSQL DDL (no markdown, no explanation) for 06_SCHEMA/137_unified_model_routing_policy.sql. Create in schema lucidota_runtime with CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS: model_routing_policy with columns: id uuid PK DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), workload_type text NOT NULL (embed|codegen|sql|reasoning|ner|analysis|orchestration), provider text NOT NULL (groq|codestral|ministral|mamba_ram|mamba_gpu|deepseek|bonsai_ternary|bge_embed|local_llama), priority smallint NOT NULL DEFAULT 5 CHECK(priority BETWEEN 1 AND 10), health text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ok' CHECK(health IN ('ok','degraded','offline')), success_rate numeric(5,4) DEFAULT 0.5000, bandit_weight numeric(8,4) DEFAULT 1.0000, latency_p50_ms integer DEFAULT NULL, cost_per_1k_tokens numeric(10,6) DEFAULT NULL, always_hot boolean DEFAULT false, vram_mb_required integer DEFAULT 0, notes text, last_updated timestamptz DEFAULT now(), UNIQUE(workload_type, provider). Also INSERT default rows for all known provider/workload combos from the LUCIDOTA fleet. Add a header comment explaining this is the single routing source of truth for all components.

label: unified-router-reader
prompt: Read /home/mfspx/LUCIDOTA/core/model_router.py and /home/mfspx/LUCIDOTA/pypeline/math/bandit_router.py. Write /home/mfspx/LUCIDOTA/scripts/lucidota_model_router.py: a unified routing client that ALL components should use. It must: (1) on init, load the full lucidota_runtime.model_routing_policy table from Postgres (LUCIDOTA_GO_STATE_DSN), (2) cache in memory with a 60-second TTL refresh, (3) expose route(workload_type: str, exclude_offline: bool=True) -> str that returns the best provider name using: first filter by health='ok', then sort by bandit_weight * success_rate DESC, (4) expose route_with_fallback(workload_type, fallback='groq') that tries primary and falls back on any error, (5) expose update_outcome(workload_type, provider, success: bool) that calls the bandit epsilon-greedy update from bandit_router.py and writes the new weight back to model_routing_policy, (6) class-level singleton pattern so all callers share one cache. Uses psycopg2. No stubs.

label: sequencer-db-routing
prompt: Read /home/mfspx/LUCIDOTA/scripts/vibe_sequencer.py and /home/mfspx/LUCIDOTA/scripts/lucidota_model_router.py (if it exists). Modify vibe_sequencer.py resolve_model() function to: (1) try to import lucidota_model_router and call ModelRouter().route(workload_type) to get the model, where workload_type is inferred from the prompt (sql->groq, typescript->codestral, rust->codestral, python->codestral, default->codestral), (2) if lucidota_model_router is not importable or Postgres is unavailable, fall back to the existing heuristic logic, (3) after execute_job() completes, call router.update_outcome(workload_type, model, success=(status=='ok')) to feed the result back into bandit weights. Wrap all router calls in try/except. Minimal changes to the existing file structure.
